title: Urostachya celebica
taxonomy: Urostachya celebica is a genus of flowering plants in the family Strelitziaceae. This genus is native to Madagascar and is known for its unique and interesting characteristics. Urostachya celebica is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and its full classification is as follows: kingdom Plantae, family Strelitziaceae, genus Urostachya. Within this genus, Urostachya celebica is the only known species. Further research may be required to confirm the accuracy of its classification.

intro: Urostachya celebica, also known as the Celebes climbing fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Strophariaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Urostachya celebica include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
